blocks.content: Ethan's plan for the day was simple.

Finish coffee. Find a painting spot. Make something decent enough to post online. Maybe get a commission inquiry. Maybe not spiral about whether his art career was just a slow-motion disaster wearing a creative hat.
A normal Wednesday.
He packed his pencils into the side pocket of his bag and stood.
Mr. Alvarez appeared beside his table with a rag in hand, though there was nothing nearby that needed wiping.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: You are leaving early.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I'm going to paint outside today.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: You say that like outside has done nothing to offend you.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: It has bugs, but the lighting is good.
Mr. Alvarez glanced toward the sketchbook.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: New project?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Maybe.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: Maybe is what artists say when they are afraid to admit they care.
Ethan slid the sketchbook into his bag.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You always this encouraging?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: I am a fountain of emotional support.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You once told me my clouds looked like mashed potatoes.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: They did. But emotionally supportive mashed potatoes.
Ethan laughed under his breath and headed for the door.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: Ethan.
He turned.
The older man nodded toward the window, toward the street where the red-haired girl had been minutes ago.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: Sometimes, a painting finds you before you find it.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I have no idea what you mean.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: That is believable only if I have recently suffered a head injury.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I'm leaving now.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: Take your coffee.
Ethan grabbed the cup he had almost forgotten and pushed out into the morning.
